results.possibleIdentity: David Stuart Leith was an electronics engineer and technical writer active during the late 1960s and early 1970s in New Jersey. He was employed by DATA Incorporated (also known as D.A.T.A., Inc. or Derivation and Tabulation Associates), a technical publishing company based in Orange and later Bloomfield, New Jersey, which specialized in electronic component data books. Leith's professional expertise included the fields of cryogenics and technical writing. He was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established in 1966 to foster collaborations between artists and engineers. Leith was part of the E.A.T. Technical Services program, which maintained a directory of engineers and scientists willing to provide technical assistance to artists for various projects.

Leith's professional expertise included the fields of cryogenics and technical writing.
He was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established in 1966 to foster collaborations between artists and engineers.
